scope: Final sweep — LOW bug triage + new issue detection post-C40b

A_zoneToast_innerHTML.detail: MerlinLairScene.ts line 846 and line 958 both assign via zoneToast.innerHTML with interpolated strings ZONE_ARIA_LABELS[zone] and ZONE_LORE[zone]. These strings are defined as Readonly<Record<LairZone, string>> literals — no user input, no external data. XSS risk is theoretical (static data) but the pattern is flagged. BUG-39-06 is confirmed still open.
A_zoneToast_innerHTML.evidence: Line 846: zoneToast.innerHTML = `${ZONE_ARIA_LABELS[zone]}${ZONE_LORE[zone]}` Line 958: same pattern in keyboard nav branch
A_zoneToast_innerHTML.recommendation: Replace innerHTML with DOM construction: create <strong> and <span> via createElement, set textContent on each, append to zoneToast. Eliminates the XSS vector pattern even if data is currently static.

B_AudioContext_per_finish.detail: MinigameBase.ts line 77: `const ctx = new AudioContext()` is called inside playOutcomeTone() which fires on every minigame completion. Each call creates a new AudioContext instance. The context is closed via setTimeout after tone playback (~0.4s), but browser AudioContext limits (typically 6 on mobile Safari) can still be hit if the player triggers multiple finishes rapidly, or if the setTimeout close races with a new creation. BUG-39-05 confirmed still open.
B_AudioContext_per_finish.evidence: Line 75-108: function playOutcomeTone() creates new AudioContext() on every call
B_AudioContext_per_finish.recommendation: Hoist AudioContext to module-level singleton (lazy-init on first call, reuse thereafter). Pattern: `let _ctx: AudioContext | null = null; function getCtx() { if (!_ctx || _ctx.state === 'closed') _ctx = new AudioContext(); return _ctx; }`

C_visibilitychange_listener.detail: MerlinLairScene.ts has no document.visibilitychange listener. The Three.js render loop (RAF-driven via the returned update() function) continues running when the browser tab is hidden or the app is backgrounded. On mobile this causes unnecessary battery drain and CPU usage for candle animations, dust motes, cauldron steam, and crystal float updates. The existing lowFpsMode guard helps when tab is visible but does not cut the loop on tab-hidden.
C_visibilitychange_listener.evidence: Grep for visibilitychange/visibilityState/document.hidden in MerlinLairScene.ts: no matches
C_visibilitychange_listener.recommendation: Add: `const onVisibility = () => { /* signal RAF host to pause when document.hidden */ }; document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', onVisibility);` and remove it in dispose(). The update() caller in the RAF host should check document.hidden and skip calling update(). Alternatively add an internal paused flag that update() checks at the top.

D_listener_leaks_in_dispose.detail: dispose() at lines 1021-1053 removes all 6 listeners registered on renderer.domElement (mousemove, click, touchmove, touchstart, touchend, keydown) and window (resize). doorFlashCancelHandle is cleared. stopAmbient() called. ariaLive and zoneToast DOM nodes removed. No listener leak detected for the listeners that are registered.
D_listener_leaks_in_dispose.caveat: The visibilitychange listener (BUG-41-03) is not registered at all, so there is no leak for it — but it also means the feature is absent.

E_render_loop_ended_guard.detail: MerlinLairScene update() function (line 973) has no early-return guard `if (lairDisposed) return`. After dispose() sets lairDisposed=true, if the RAF host fires one more frame before it cancels the animation frame, update() will execute — including renderer.render(), dust.update(), cauldron.update(), and lairWindow.update() on already-disposed resources. In practice this is usually harmless (renderer.render on a disposed renderer throws silently) but it is a latent crash risk on slow devices where RAF timing is irregular.
E_render_loop_ended_guard.evidence: Grep for `if.*ended.*return` and `lairDisposed.*return` in MerlinLairScene.ts: no matches. update() function starts at line 973 with no guard.
E_render_loop_ended_guard.recommendation: Add `if (lairDisposed) return;` as the first line of update().

mg_traces.detail: BUG-39-08 verified fixed. Timer bar id is 'mg-traces-timer' (line 44) and fill id is 'mg-traces-timer-fill' (line 53). tieredValue() used for totalTime/footprintCount/hitRadius (lines 32-34). ended flag present (line 26), reset in setup() line 72. No regressions detected.

mg_volonte.detail: BUG-39-10 verified fixed. totalTime field is no longer readonly (line 38 comment explicitly notes 'was readonly, blocked scaling'). tieredValue() applied to totalTime/targetRadius/spawnInterval/maxDistractors (lines 63-66). ended flag present (line 53). No regressions detected.

mg_combat_rituel.detail: BUG-39-04 verified fixed. tieredValue() used for totalTime [16,14,12,10] and obstacleCount [3,4,5,6] (lines 59-60). Comment on line 58 confirms: 'C103: tieredValue replaces manual arithmetic'. ended flag present (line 29). No regressions detected.

issues.issue: zoneToast.innerHTML used with interpolated strings at lines 846 and 958 of MerlinLairScene.ts
issues.recommendation: Replace both innerHTML assignments with createElement + textContent DOM construction

issues.issue: new AudioContext() instantiated per minigame finish in MinigameBase.ts playOutcomeTone(). Risks hitting browser AudioContext limit on rapid completions or mobile Safari.
issues.recommendation: Module-level singleton AudioContext with lazy-init and state check before reuse

issues.issue: No document.visibilitychange listener in MerlinLairScene. Render loop runs at full rate when tab is hidden, wasting battery/CPU on mobile.
issues.recommendation: Add visibilitychange listener to pause update() when document.hidden is true; remove in dispose()

issues.issue: MerlinLairScene update() has no `if (lairDisposed) return` guard. A stale RAF frame after dispose() may call renderer.render() on disposed resources.
issues.recommendation: Add `if (lairDisposed) return;` as first line of update() function

build_score.rationale: 0 HIGH bugs, 0 CRITICAL, 1 MEDIUM (AudioContext singleton — low actual risk in practice due to auto-close), 3 LOW (2 new: visibility pause + dispose guard; 1 carry-forward: innerHTML). All 14 minigames have tieredValue + ended guard coverage confirmed. C40b fixes verified clean. Build is releasable. LOW bugs should be resolved in next maintenance cycle but do not block.

next_steps: BUG-41-01 (LOW): Replace zoneToast.innerHTML with DOM construction in MerlinLairScene.ts lines 846 and 958
BUG-41-02 (MED): Hoist AudioContext to module singleton in MinigameBase.ts
BUG-41-03 (LOW): Add visibilitychange pause listener to MerlinLairScene + remove in dispose()
BUG-41-04 (LOW): Add `if (lairDisposed) return;` guard at top of update() in MerlinLairScene.ts
